# criblio_parquet_schema (Resource)

ParquetSchema Resource

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "criblio_parquet_schema" "my_parquet_schema" {
  description = "Parquet schema for structured event data"
  group_id = "default"
  id = "events-parquet-schema"
  schema = "{\"schema\":[{\"name\":\"timestamp\",\"type\":\"INT64\",\"logicalType\":\"TIMESTAMP_MILLIS\"},{\"name\":\"source\",\"type\":\"BYTE_ARRAY\",\"logicalType\":\"UTF8\"},{\"name\":\"message\",\"type\":\"BYTE_ARRAY\",\"logicalType\":\"UTF8\"},{\"name\":\"severity\",\"type\":\"INT32\"}]}"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tools/codegen -->
## Schema

### Required

- `group_id` (String) Worker group ID.
- `id` (String)
- `schema` (String) JSON schema matching standards of draft version 2019-09

### Optional

- `description` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = criblio_parquet_schema.my_criblio_parquet_schema
  id = jsonencode({
    group_id = "Cribl"
    id       = "schema-logs"
  })
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import criblio_parquet_schema.my_criblio_parquet_schema '{"group_id": "Cribl", "id": "schema-logs"}'
```
